text: Six months post-colectomy, the surgeon performs a circumferential incision around the stoma site in the left lower quadrant. The loop ileostomy is released, temporarily sealed with a trio-viral stick, and returned to the abdomen. Pneumoperitoneum is established using a single port access device.

The surgeon ligates the inferior mesenteric artery at the level of the superior artery and dissects through the avascular plane posterior to the mesorectum, continuing down to the levator muscles. Dissection is carried out bilaterally upon reaching the levator muscles, followed by anterior mobilization. After achieving complete mobilization, a digital rectal exam confirms the dissection level. The laparoscopic GIA-XVI stapler is fired twice to staple the rectum.

A 15 to 20 cm ileal pouch is created using a stapling device. A leak test is performed, showing no leaks. A purse-string suture is placed with 2-0 Prolene, and an EEA-28 stapler is inserted into the pouch. Under laparoscopic view, an intracorporeal anastomosis is created, followed by a pouchoscopy. A loop of bowel proximal to the pouch is exteriorized, and the ileostomy is matured.
